## Venue

- **Primary target**: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
- **Alternative**: Reliability Engineering & System Safety
- **Rationale**: Core MLE methodology paper for reliability engineering audience

## Thesis

This paper provides the complete Weibull treatment for masked series systems: explicit score equations, Fisher information matrices, comprehensive simulation studies, and practical estimation guidance for both homogeneous (common shape) and heterogeneous (distinct shapes) Weibull components.

## Phase

- **Current**: `idea` (planned, referenced in foundation paper; much infrastructure exists in maskedcauses)
- **Next action**: Derive explicit score equations for homogeneous and heterogeneous Weibull
- **Blocking**: None

## Outline

1. Introduction — gap between general framework and Weibull specifics
2. Preliminaries — Weibull series model, C1-C2-C3 specialization
3. Homogeneous Weibull (m+1 params) — closure theorem, time-invariant cause probs, score equations, FIM
4. Heterogeneous Weibull (2m params) — time-varying cause probs, numerical integration, score/Hessian
5. Comparison — bias-variance tradeoff, computational cost, model selection guidance
6. Simulation study — bias, MSE, coverage under varying masking/censoring/n
7. Case study — realistic synthetic or real data example
8. Discussion — sensitivity to starting values, bootstrap vs asymptotic CI

## What Already Exists

- `maskedcauses` R package implements all three model levels (exponential, homogeneous, heterogeneous)
- `vignettes/framework.Rmd` walks through the theory with code
- Model selection paper has extensive LRT simulations for hom/het comparison
- expo-masked-fim has the exponential Fisher information

## Foundation Paper Citation

```bibtex
@article{towell2025masked,
  author  = {Towell, Alexander},
  title   = {Masked Causes of Failure in Series Systems: A Likelihood Framework},
  year    = {2025},
  note    = {DOI: pending},
  url     = {https://github.com/queelius/masked-causes-in-series-systems}
}
```

**Specific results cited:**
| Foundation Result | Used In (this paper) | How |
|-------------------|----------------------|-----|
| Conditions C1-C2-C3 (Sec 5) | Sec 2 | Framework assumptions |
| Combined likelihood (Theorem 9) | Sec 3-4 | Specialized to Weibull hazard |
| Theorem 10 (log-likelihood) | Sec 3-4 | Score equations derived from this |
| Identifiability Theorem | Sec 2 | Conditions under which Weibull MLE exists |
| Common Hazard Specifications — Weibull (Sec 7) | Sec 3-4 | Starting point; this paper develops the full MLE treatment |

**Relationship**: Heavy dependency — this paper instantiates the general framework for Weibull, deriving everything the foundation paper leaves abstract (score equations, FIM, asymptotics, simulations).

## Related Work (Ecosystem)

### Expo-Masked-FIM (`~/github/papers/expo-masked-fim/`)
- **Status**: Review (v3.2.0)
- **Role**: Exponential special case — provides the analytical baseline. This paper extends the FIM derivation approach from exponential to Weibull, where closed forms may still exist for the homogeneous case.

### Model Selection Paper (`~/github/papers/masked-series-model-selection/`)
- **Status**: Draft complete
- **Role**: Cross-reference for hom/het comparison (don't duplicate). LRT methodology and power analysis already done there. This paper focuses on estimation, not selection.

### Master's Project (`~/github/papers/reliability-estimation-in-series-systems/`)
- **Role**: Origin. Weibull simulation infrastructure established there.

### Foundation Paper (`~/github/papers/masked-causes-in-series-systems/`)
- **Role**: Heavy dependency. Score equations derived from the general log-likelihood (Theorem 10).

### Sibling Companions
- **01-identifiability**: FIM decomposition framework applies to Weibull case.
- **04-nesting-vs-structure**: This paper provides the estimation theory for the upper nesting levels that paper discusses methodologically.

### R Packages
- `maskedcauses` (v0.9): Implements all three model levels (expo, hom-Weibull, het-Weibull). Score, Hessian, and MLE already coded.
- `compositional.mle` (v1.0.2): Numerical optimization for heterogeneous Weibull MLE.

## Open Questions

- Closed-form Fisher information for homogeneous Weibull?
- Profile likelihood for shape parameter under heavy masking?
- Bootstrap vs asymptotic confidence intervals: when does asymptotic break down?
